title: 10 best herbal essential oils for body odor
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for body odor are natural remedies derived from plants that help eliminate unpleasant body smells by reducing bacteria and odor-causing microorganisms on the skin. These oils have antibacterial and antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria, effectively eliminating body odor. The best herbal essential oils for body odor are Melaleuca alternifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, Lavandula angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Cymbopogon nardus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Zingiber officinale, Cymbopogon martini, and Cinnamomum camphora. If left untreated, body odor can severely impact one's social and professional life, causing anxiety, low self-esteem, and social isolation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial properties that target and eliminate bacteria responsible for causing body odor. When applied topically or added to bathwater, tea tree oil creates an environment that is inhospitable to the growth of odor-causing microorganisms. Additionally, its deodorizing properties help neutralize existing odors, leaving skin feeling fresh and clean. As a result, tea tree essential oils provide a natural and effective way to combat body odor and leave you feeling confident and comfortable in your own skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate bacteria that cause body odor, such as Corynebacterium and Staphylococcus aureus.
Deodorizing properties: Tea tree oil's deodorizing properties neutralize sweat and reduce the formation of compounds responsible for unpleasant odors, leaving skin feeling fresh and clean.

Antifungal properties: Tea tree oil's antifungal properties help to prevent fungal overgrowth, which can contribute to body odor issues by breaking down sweat and producing compounds with unpleasant smells.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial properties, tea tree leaves are used to create essential oil that helps combat bacteria and fungi causing body odor.
Buds: The young buds of the tea tree plant contain high levels of terpenes, which provide a natural deodorizing effect and help reduce sweat-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Steam distill the cleaned leaves in a stainless steel apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 3 hours.
Collect the yielded essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in cool places.
Label each bottle with the date of extraction and use it within 6 months or add a few drops to your bath water for immediate relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria, which is a primary cause of body odor. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can help reduce sweat gland activity, leading to less moisture and a decrease in the amount of substances that contribute to body odor. Furthermore, lavender's deodorizing properties can freshen and purify the skin, leaving it feeling clean and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender oil's antimicrobial properties help to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause body odor, reducing the need for frequent deodorant or antiperspirant applications.

Antifungal properties: Lavender oil's antifungal properties combat fungal overgrowth in the body, which is a common contributor to body odor and sweat-based infections like athlete's foot and candidiasis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, irritation, and itching associated with skin conditions that can contribute to body odor, such as eczema and acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective in controlling body odor.

Flowers: The flowers of the English lavender plant are rich in linalool, a compound that has been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air drying method.
Create a botanical mixture by combining dried lavender flowers with a carrier oil.
Steep the botanical mixture in a warm dark place for several weeks.
Filter and bottle the resulting essential oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The oil's citral and geraniol compounds work together to reduce bacteria growth on the skin, which is a common cause of body odor. Additionally, lemongrass essential oil has a deodorizing effect, helping to eliminate unpleasant sweat smells. As a result, incorporating lemongrass essential oil into one's daily routine can leave skin feeling fresher and more confident throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass essential oil has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacteria and fungi that cause body odor by reducing their growth and activity.
Deodorizing properties: The oil's deodorizing properties can help eliminate or reduce unpleasant body odor by neutralizing sweat and controlling the growth of bacteria that cause odors.

Antifungal properties: Lemongrass essential oil has antifungal properties, which can help prevent the growth of fungi that can contribute to body odor, especially in areas prone to moisture like armpits and feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots are prized for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating bacterial infections that cause body odor.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, a type of underground stem, contains compounds that help to reduce sweat and eliminate bacteria that contribute to body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning for optimal aroma and potency.
Cleanly chop 500g of harvested leaves into small pieces using scissors or pruning shears.
Steam distill the chopped leaves in a still for 4 hours at a temperature range of 80-100°c.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil from the distillation process using coffee filters or cotton cloth.
Store the final essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep them refrigerated to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural antibacterial, antifungal, and deodorizing properties. When applied topically to the skin, rosemary oil can help eliminate bacteria that cause sweat to become smelly. It also has a pleasant, herbaceous scent that masks unpleasant odors, leaving the body smelling fresh and clean. Additionally, rosemary oil's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of microorganisms that contribute to body odor.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms that can cause body odor. This reduces the amount of odor-causing compounds produced by these organisms.

Antifungal properties: Rosemary essential oil's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections such as athlete's foot or ringworm, which can contribute to unpleasant body odors. By reducing fungal growth, rosemary essential oil helps prevent these infections from causing bad smells.

Deodorizing and astringent properties: Rosemary essential oil has deodorizing and astringent properties that help eliminate existing body odor and reduce sweat production. This leaves the skin feeling fresh and clean, with reduced likelihood of new odors forming.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant are rich in antioxidants and camphor, which have natural antibacterial properties that help combat body odor.
Stems: Rosemary stems contain a high concentration of verbenone, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce sweat and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when they are at their highest concentration of fragrance compounds.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 500ml of water at 100°c for 30 minutes to extract the essential oil.
Separate the resulting essential oil from the water using a glass separating funnel and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 24 hours before use, shaking the bottle occasionally to ensure even distribution of fragrance compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial and antifungal properties. These properties help to reduce the bacteria that thrive on the skin's surface, causing unpleasant odors. The oil also has astringent and antiseptic qualities that help to dry out the skin and reduce sweat production, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing body odor. Additionally, pot marigold essential oils have been known to help soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold essential oil exhibits antimicrobial activity against a wide range of bacteria, fungi, and yeast, helping to reduce the growth of microorganisms that can cause body odor.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold essential oil help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with sweat and body odor.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold essential oil's antioxidant activity helps neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to unpleasant body odors, promoting healthy skin and a fresh scent.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antibacterial and antifungal properties, roots help combat bacteria and fungi that contribute to body odor.
Seeds: Containing antimicrobial compounds, seeds provide a natural defense against microorganisms that cause unpleasant odors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak blooming stage for optimal potency and sweet aroma.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area or using desiccants for 7-10 days.
Steam distill dried calendula flowers with water in a ratio of 1:4 at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect and filter the essential oil from distillation condensate using coffee filters or cotton cloths.
Store the resulting calendula essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which effectively combat bacteria and fungi that cause unpleasant sweat and body odor. When applied topically or diffused in the air, thyme oil creates a protective barrier on the skin, preventing the growth of these microorganisms and reducing the occurrence of body odor. Additionally, its deodorizing properties help to eliminate existing odors, leaving you feeling fresher and more confident throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Thyme essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and protozoa that cause body odor, helping to eliminate these microorganisms and reduce unpleasant odors.

Antifungal property: Thyme essential oil's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections like athlete's foot and ringworm, which can contribute to body odor by causing excessive sweating or skin irritation.
Deodorizing property: Thyme essential oil has a natural deodorizing effect that helps neutralize unpleasant odors caused by sweat and bacteria, leaving the body smelling fresh and clean.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are valued for their earthy, grounding properties that help balance and purify the body's natural scent.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain high concentrations of antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacteria and fungi causing body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier weighing approximately 500 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm air circulation oven at 35 degrees celsius for six hours to preserve quality and potency.
Crush the dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle taking about 30 minutes to complete.
Steam distill the powdered thymus vulgaris leaves with distilled water in a copper still for four hours at atmospheric pressure extracting the essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, yielding approximately 20 milliliters of pure thymus vulgaris essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal essential oils helps with body odor because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which work to eliminate bacteria that cause unpleasant smells. The oil's active compound, berberine, has been shown to inhibit the growth of these bacteria, reducing their ability to break down sweat and produce odorous compounds. Additionally, goldenseal's natural astringent properties help to tighten pores, allowing for more effective sweating and reduced moisture buildup, further contributing to a fresher, cleaner scent.
remedy_properties: Tea tree oil (Melaleuca alternifolia): Has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate bacteria that cause body odor.
Lavender oil (Lavandula angustifolia): May help reduce sweat production and promote a sense of freshness and cleanliness.
Geranium oil (Pelargonium graveolens): May help balance hormone levels, which can contribute to body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural antibacterial agent that helps combat body odor-causing bacteria.
Barks: Containing berberine and other bioactive compounds, barks are effective at reducing sweat and body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh hydrastis canadensis roots and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and soak them in 4 cups of vodka for 48 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place for 14 days.
Filter the liquid again and dilute it with distilled water to create a 10% essential oil solution.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural deodorizing properties. The oil's menthol content has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce sweat production and eliminate bacteria that cause unpleasant odors. Additionally, peppermint oil's refreshing and cooling sensation can also help to increase blood circulation, promoting a healthy skin environment that is less conducive to body odor. This natural remedy can be used in combination with other antiperspirants or deodorants for added effectiveness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Peppermint essential oil's antimicrobial properties help kill bacteria that cause body odor, reducing the production of odor-causing compounds.
Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to body odor.
Cooling and decongestant property: Peppermint essential oil's cooling and decongestant properties help reduce sweat production, which can reduce the occurrence of body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce sweat and body odor, making them a popular choice for aromatherapy.
Leaves: The leaves of peppermint contain high amounts of menthone, which has natural deodorizing properties and helps to eliminate bacteria that cause body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a pesticide-free garden or farm on a sunny day.
Dry the freshly harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 8 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried peppermint leaves in 500ml of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or sedimentation method within 2 hours.
Store the extracted mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which work to combat bacteria that cause unpleasant odors. The oil's compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing sweat-induced odor-causing agents. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils' deodorizing properties help to neutralize existing body odor, leaving the skin feeling refreshed and confident.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: This property helps to eliminate bacteria and other microorganisms that cause body odor by inhibiting their growth and reproduction.

Decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce sweat gland activity, which in turn reduces the amount of odor-causing compounds produced by bacteria on the skin.
Antioxidant properties: As an antioxidant, Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to body odor by breaking down proteins and fats on the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tasmanian Blue Gum roots have antimicrobial properties that help to control bacterial growth, reducing body odor.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, helping to reduce sweat and body odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eucalyptus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated and before heat of the day sets in weighing approximately two cups per batch.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus leaves using a food dehydrator on low setting for six hours or air dry them for up to three weeks ensuring they are brittle and crumbly.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus leaves in a copper still with a ratio of one part eucalyptus to two parts water for 30-45 minutes collecting the essential oil as it separates.
Filter the collected essential oil using a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any remaining solids and sediment taking care not to contaminate the oil.

Store the filtered herbal eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place for up to six months while ensuring it is kept away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically or diffused into the air, wormwood oil works to eliminate bacteria that cause body odor, leaving skin feeling fresher and cleaner. Additionally, its deodorizing effects help neutralize sweat and reduce perspiration, making it an effective solution for individuals who experience excessive sweating or strong body odor. This natural remedy provides a safe and gentle way to address this common issue.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, yeast, and fungi, which can cause body odor by breaking down sweat and producing unpleasant compounds.

Antiperspirant and antifungal properties: Wormwood oil's ability to reduce sweating and prevent fungal overgrowth helps to eliminate the underlying causes of body odor, making it an effective natural remedy for preventing and treating foot and groin fungus.

Deodorizing and antiseptic properties: The deodorizing properties of wormwood essential oil help to neutralize unpleasant body odors, while its antiseptic properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against body odor-causing bacteria.
Barks: Containing aromatic compounds like thujone and camphor, which help to deodorize and freshen the skin, reducing sweat-induced odors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves in large quantities ensuring a minimum of 250 grams for the recipe.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water removing any dirt or debris and pat dry with a clean cloth.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature range of 100 to 150 degrees celsius.
Collect the resulting essential oil which should have a dark yellow to amber color and a sweet earthy aroma and filter it using a coffee filter or cheesecloth.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight fitting lid for a minimum of 48 hours allowing the flavors to mature.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat body odor with herbal essential oils is to combine them in a blend that targets sweat glands. Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties reduce bacteria growth, while lemongrass oil's deodorizing effects eliminate odors. Lavender oil's soothing qualities calm the skin, and geranium oil's antifungal properties prevent fungal infections. Apply this blend topically after showering or bathing for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to body odor that are treated with herbal essential oils are acne, athlete's foot, and vaginal yeast infections. Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that help combat acne, while lemongrass oil has antifungal properties that can alleviate fungal infections like athlete's foot. Geranium oil has antimicrobial properties that can help treat vaginal yeast infections. These essential oils can be used in combination with other natural remedies to promote healthy skin and body functions.

This results in a decrease in the production of volatile compounds responsible for unpleasant odors, leaving the skin feeling fresh and clean. remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural deodorizing and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as limonene and beta-pinene, work to combat the bacteria that cause body odor, keeping the skin's natural pH balanced. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cymbopogon citratus are primarily used to make essential oils for body odor due to their high citronella content, which provides a natural and refreshing deodorizing effect.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus are used to produce essential oils for body odor because they contain compounds that help to reduce sweat and kill bacteria that cause body odor.

Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used for essential oil production for body odor, as they contain citronella and other compounds that help to eliminate body odor-causing bacteria and provide a fresh scent.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus at dawn when the plant is at its peak oil production.
Wash the harvested leaves with cool water to remove dirt and other contaminants from the surface.
Steam distill 100g of the leaves for 3-4 hours to extract the essential oil from the plant material.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel or decanter.
Store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with body odor because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin. Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has antifungal properties, which help to prevent the growth of fungi that can cause body odor.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary source of essential oils due to their high concentration of linalool, a compound known for its deodorizing and antibacterial properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the lavandula angustifolia flowers when they are in full bloom and the buds are just starting to open.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper bags or trays for about 2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried flowers at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius for about 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the water through condensation and separation equipment.
Filter the essential oil using a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any impurities.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves from the plant, selecting those with the highest eucalyptus globulus content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a 1:5 leaf-to-water ratio at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled steam and separate it from the water using a condenser.
Filter the distilled steam through a filter paper to remove any remaining plant particles.
Store the resulting essential oil in a dark glass container away from heat sources at a temperature of 15-20 degrees celsius.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 kg of cymbopogon nardus leaves and stems when the plant is in full bloom for optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-150 mmhg pressure for 2-3 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water through decantation or settling for 30 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any impurities and contaminants for 15 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids away from heat and light sources.

Furthermore, its deodorizing properties help to neutralize and eliminate unpleasant body odor-causing compounds, promoting a fresher and more confident you. Regular use can lead to a significant reduction in body odor.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: Helps with body odor by its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for causing bad smells.
Bisabolol: Aids in reducing body odor by its anti-inflammatory properties, which prevent the irritation of skin and reduce sweat production.
Gingerol: Contributes to controlling body odor by its deodorizing properties, which neutralize the volatile organic compounds (VOCs) that cause unpleasant smells.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the primary source of essential oils for body odor due to their high concentration of zingiberene, which has antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
Stems: Stems of Zingiber officinale contain essential oils that help control body odor by reducing the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause sweat odor.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to produce essential oils that help in controlling body odor by inhibiting the growth of odor-causing microorganisms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale at the peak of maturity usually after 8-10 months of plantation.
Dry the fresh rhizomes in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days at a temperature of 30-40°c.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes at a temperature of 80-100°c for 3-4 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator or decanter to obtain the final product.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a paper filter or a glass filter to remove any impurities and obtain clear oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon martini essential oils helps with body odor because of its natural deodorizing and antimicrobial properties. The oil, commonly known as East Indian lemongrass oil, contains compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which contribute to body odor. Its citrusy scent also helps to mask unpleasant smells, leaving a refreshing and invigorating aroma. Additionally, it has been traditionally used to reduce sweat and promote a sense of cleanliness, making it a popular choice for people looking to manage body odor naturally.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: It has antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause body odor.
Limonene: It possesses deodorizing and antifungal properties, which help neutralize and eliminate the causes of body odor.
Zingiberene: It has a deodorizing effect, helping to eliminate unpleasant odors and reduce the production of sweat that can cause body odor.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of citronella oil, which has natural deodorizing properties.
Leaves: They contain lemongrass oil, which is known for its ability to repel insects and reduce body odor.
Stems: They are also used to produce lemongrass essential oil, which is often used in perfumes and deodorants for its refreshing scent.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of fresh cymbopogon martini leaves and 1l of distilled water for the infusion process.
Steam distill the gathered leaves for 3 hours to separate the essential oils from the plant material.
Collect the distilled essential oils and filter them through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oils in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it properly.
Inspect the stored essential oils for any signs of spoilage or contamination after 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum camphora essential oils helps with body odor because of its deodorizing properties. It has natural antimicrobial and antifungal abilities that inhibit the growth of bacteria, which can cause body odor. The oil's camphor content helps to dry the skin and reduce moisture, making it an ideal solution for individuals with excessive sweating. Additionally, its refreshing and cooling scent provides a sense of invigoration, leaving the skin feeling fresh and clean. Regular use can help to minimize body odor and promote a sense of confidence.
remedy_constituents: Camphene: Helps to reduce and eliminate body odor by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause unpleasant smells.
Bornyl acetate: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, killing bacteria and fungi that contribute to body odor.
Limonene: Possesses deodorizing properties, helping to neutralize and eliminate unpleasant body odors.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make essential oils for body odor because they contain camphor, a natural deodorizer and antiseptic.
Barks: Used to make essential oils for body odor because they contain aromatic compounds that help repel and eliminate odor-causing bacteria.
Stems: Used to make essential oils for body odor because they contain oils that help reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth.
